# API, Data, Security, and Performance Checklist: Generate 7-Day Demand Forecast

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, and completeness of UC-04 requirements for API, data, security, and performance concerns before implementation.
**Created**: 2026-03-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](/home/asiad/ece493/311-forecast-system/specs/004-weekly-demand-forecast/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## API Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK001 Are all forecast API surfaces and their responsibilities explicitly specified for trigger, run-status, and current-weekly-forecast retrieval? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, FR-002, FR-013; Plan §Implementation Steps 2, 8; Contract §Paths] - PASS
- [x] CHK002 Are API expectations consistent about when a request reuses an existing weekly forecast versus generating a new one? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008, FR-014; Spec §User Story 2; Plan §Implementation Step 4] - PASS
- [x] CHK003 Are outcome vocabularies for accepted run states and API error states defined without ambiguity across spec and contract artifacts? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009-FR-012; Data Model §Entity: WeeklyForecastRun; Contract §WeeklyForecastRunStatus] - FOLLOW-UP
- [x] CHK004 Are response requirements for unauthorized, forbidden, not-found, and invalid-request cases defined for each API surface or explicitly marked out of scope? [Gap, Contract §Paths; Spec §Requirements] - PASS

## Data Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK005 Are data entities and relationships specified clearly enough to distinguish run records, stored forecast versions, bucket rows, and the active weekly marker? [Completeness, Spec §Key Entities; Data Model §Entity: WeeklyForecastRun, WeeklyForecastVersion, WeeklyForecastBucket, CurrentWeeklyForecastMarker] - PASS
- [x] CHK006 Are identity and uniqueness expectations defined for "one current forecast per operational week" without conflicting interpretations? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007, FR-008, FR-014; Data Model §Derived Invariants] - PASS
- [x] CHK007 Are week-boundary requirements fully specified (timezone basis, start/end rules, and crossover handling) so weekly coverage is objectively determinable? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications; Spec §FR-014; Data Model §Entity: WeeklyForecastRun] - FOLLOW-UP
- [x] CHK008 Are requirements explicit about historical retention scope for run outcomes and forecast versions, or intentionally scoped out with rationale? [Gap, Spec §FR-007, FR-012; Plan §Phase 0 Research Summary; Data Model §Derived Invariants] - PASS

## Security Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK009 Are authorization requirements specified per operation (trigger vs read) with enough detail to avoid role ambiguity? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions & Dependencies; Plan §Implementation Step 2] - FOLLOW-UP
- [x] CHK010 Are data-protection requirements defined for logs and API payloads, including explicit limits on sensitive operational or model details? [Completeness, Plan §Constraints; Plan §Implementation Step 8] - PASS
- [x] CHK011 Are security failure classes (unauthorized/forbidden/invalid request) consistently separated from business forecast failure classes in written requirements? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009-FR-012; Plan §Implementation Step 2; Data Model §Entity: WeeklyForecastRun] - PASS
- [x] CHK012 Does the specification define whether access-control denials should be included or excluded from run-history records? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-012; Data Model §Validation rules for WeeklyForecastRun; Gap] - PASS

## Performance Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK013 Are performance targets quantified for all critical weekly flows (new generation, current-forecast reuse, and scheduled execution)? [Completeness, Spec §SC-002, SC-004; Plan §Performance Goals] - PASS
- [x] CHK014 Are performance requirements explicit about which targets apply only to successful outcomes versus all terminal outcomes? [Clarity, Spec §SC-001-SC-004; Plan §Performance Goals] - PASS
- [x] CHK015 Are load/concurrency requirements documented for overlapping scheduled and on-demand requests, or explicitly deferred? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases; Plan §Scale/Scope] - PASS
- [x] CHK016 Are degradation expectations defined for partial-data scenarios (for example category-only fallback) with measurable timing or completion criteria? [Coverage, Spec §FR-006; Spec §Edge Cases; Spec §SC-005] - FOLLOW-UP

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
